ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-30-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert J. Silverman for Michael J. Salter, Administrator (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding depositing funds into a blocked account. Mr. Silverman confirmed that a revised proposed order for deposit had been submitted and that the administrator was prepared to make the deposit. The Court confirmed receipt of both the revised proposed order for the deposit into a blocked account and the revised order approving the first and final account.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant. A compliance hearing was scheduled for June 25, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, for filing of receipt of deposit into a blocked account.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Continued To Feb-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert J. Silverman for Michael J. Salter, Administrator (via Zoom).
Christopher Jew, Guardian Ad Litem for Anthony James Stevens and Melody Jessica Stevens, Minor Beneficiaries (via Zoom).

Mr. Silverman reported that the home in the estate ha been sold. The court-approved settlement has been implemented, and the settlement distribution was sent out recently. The $5 million creditor's claim is expected to be withdrawn within a week. The final petition is approximately 95% complete and will be filed within one to two weeks.

The court continued the matter to February 11, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel was informed that the hearing will be vacated if the final petition is filed prior to that date.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-11-2024 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Approval Of Stipulated Settlement
Off Calendar Jun-06-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Silverman appeared by zoom for petitioner.
Christopher Jew (Guardian Ad Litem) appeared by zoom for Anthony Stevens and Melody Stevens.
Michael Low appeared by zoom for Elvine Valladolid (creditor).

Discussion was held regarding the civil lawsuit against the decedent, the medical expenses, the injuries, the damages, etc. Mr. Jew stated that he had not been able to speak with Melody because she has been AWOL for a significant amount of time, that Melody has not been residing in the home, that Anthony understands and is working on moving in with his father, and that he has not been able to speak with the grandmother. Mr. Jew also stated that he is in favor of the settlement, that the civil claims are quite significant and that it is in the best interest of his clients to contain the claim. The court waives non-compliance with 14.43(D)(1)-(4). The Petition for Approval of Stipulated Settlement is under submission with the court's intent to grant.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Tan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Petition To Approve Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-30-2024 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-15-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Ricky Gonzalez and Anthony Stevens, in court, in propria persona, interested parties.

Robert Silverman, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Michael Salter, Administrator.

Michael Low, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Elvine Vallodolid, interested party.

At the outset, there is discussion regarding the court's intent to appoint Mr. Jew as guardian ad litem for the minors. No objection to the preceding is presented. The court states for the record that it is appointing Mr. Jew as guardian ad litem for minors Anthony Stevens and Melody Stevens. Mr. Silverman reports that the related civil matter has been settled, and the plan is to file a petition to approve the settlement with the probate department. Mr. Jew offers an update regarding the real property issue. The court encourages the parties to resolve that issue.

After additional discussion, the Status of Administration is taken under submission with the intent to grant. A Status on Petition to Approve Settlement is set for Apr-30-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Prado. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-02-2024 Continued To Feb-13-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Ricky Gonzalez and Anthony Stevens, in court, in propria persona, interested parties.

Frederick Barry, in court, in propria persona, interested party.

Jonathan Jaffe, Esq., via Zoom, for Michael Salter, Administrator.

Michael Low, Esq., via Zoom, for Elvine Vallodolid, interested party.

At the outset, the court reminds Mr. Gonzalez that unless he is an attorney, the Court cannot allow him to appear, file pleadings, or make oral argument for his 17-year-old child Anthony Stevens because the Court views those acts as unauthorized practice of law which is prohibited. Mr. Jaffe reports as to the status of the estate and the related civil action and that he anticipates that the estate's twenty-five (25) percent interest in the real property will be sold. Mr. Low reports that there is a tentative settlement of the civil action which may be confirmed soon. There is discussion regarding minor Melody Stevens and whether a guardian should be appointed. Mr. Gonzalez presents a letter from the Minors' proposed guardian, which the court reads on the record. The court advises that it intends to continue the subject matter. Mr. Gonzalez is advised that he should consult with an attorney and the and that the court will not strike his objection at this time, as he is not an attorney as previously stated by the court, to allow him to seek legal counsel. Mr. Berry is allowed to address the court but as with Mr. Gonzalez, the court confirms that Mr. Berry is not an attorney and therefore, the court cannot consider his statements.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Feb-13-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Jaime/Reed. Not Reported.
